text.title: Risk Threshold Policy Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the risk-threshold-policy methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Policy must partition the severity scale into 3-5 explicit bands (e.g. green ≤6, amber 7-15, red 16-25). Continuous-score 'use judgement' routing is forbidden.

rationale.source: PMBOK 7 risk governance; APM Body of Knowledge

rule.rationale: Bands convert continuous scores into discrete escalation decisions; a PM at 11pm should see 'amber → notify sponsor by EOD Friday' without judgement.

rule.statement: Each band routes to a NAMED individual (role + person), not a group inbox or committee. Fallback (deputy) named by person.
rule.rationale: Named targets are accountable; groups diffuse.

rule.statement: Each band has an explicit SLA: red ≤2h, amber ≤24h, green = weekly digest. Bands without SLA are forbidden.
rule.rationale: SLAs convert 'I'll escalate when I get to it' into a binding promise.

rule.statement: Each band defines explicit de-escalation criteria. Without criteria, risks ratchet upward and never close.
rule.rationale: A risk register without de-escalation rules becomes a museum of past worries.

rule.statement: Policy is co-signed by sponsor + delivery lead + PMO at engagement kickoff. Refresh at every milestone OR every 12 weeks, whichever first.
rule.rationale: When a red risk arrives, the sponsor cannot claim they did not know they were the escalation target.

list.item: Sponsor refuses to sign — raise the issue before delivery starts
